# zstack_port_forwarding_rule (Resource)

Manage ZStack port forwarding rules. Port forwarding rules map VIP ports to VM instance private ports, supporting TCP and UDP protocols.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the port forwarding rule.
- `protocol_type` (String) The protocol type: TCP or UDP.
- `vip_port_start` (Number) The start port on the VIP side.
- `vip_uuid` (String) The UUID of the VIP used for port forwarding.

### Optional

- `allowed_cidr` (String) The CIDR block allowed to access this port forwarding rule (e.g., 0.0.0.0/0).
- `description` (String) The description of the port forwarding rule.
- `private_port_end` (Number) The end port on the private (VM) side. Defaults to the same as vip_port_end.
- `private_port_start` (Number) The start port on the private (VM) side. Defaults to the same as vip_port_start.
- `vip_port_end` (Number) The end port on the VIP side. Defaults to the same as vip_port_start.
- `vm_nic_uuid` (String) The UUID of the VM NIC to which the port forwarding rule is attached.

### Read-Only

- `guest_ip` (String) The IP address of the guest VM.
- `state` (String) The state of the port forwarding rule (Enabled, Disabled).
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the port forwarding rule.
- `vip_ip` (String) The IP address of the VIP.
